stories.title: U.S. Women's Open Final Round — Korda vs. Kim at Riviera, Talley in Mix
stories.angle: Korda and Kim share lead at -6 heading into Sunday; Korda birdied final 3 holes in R3 despite "funky grip"; 17-year-old amateur Asterisk Talley shot record 66 on Saturday; Charley Hull 65 lurking; who wins the first USWO at Riviera?

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
USWO Final Round is here.

Korda and Kim tied at -6.
Talley (17-year-old amateur) in the mix.
Hull (65 Saturday) lurking.

The first U.S. Women's Open at Riviera. Sunday. Let's go. #USWO2026 #LPGA

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Nelly Korda came into Saturday with a "funky grip."

She left with a share of the lead, after birdying 16, 17, and 18.

That's what No. 1 players do. USWO Final Round is underway. #NellyKorda #USWO2026

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 AM ET

The 2026 U.S. Women's Open final round is underway at Riviera Country Club — and the leaderboard could not be more compelling.

Nelly Korda and Sei Young Kim share the lead at 6 under par heading into Sunday. Korda made the most dramatic move of the tournament on Saturday afternoon, birdying the final three holes — Nos. 16, 17, and 18 — to erase a deficit and seize her share of the top spot. She was the first to admit her ball-striking was off ("funky grip" was her description), but her putter was flawless when it mattered.

Kim, a proven major champion, is equally capable of holding that lead through a Sunday at Riviera.

Then there is Charley Hull, who carded the low round of the week on Saturday — a 6-under 65 — to put herself squarely in contention. And somewhere on the leaderboard, a 17-year-old amateur named Asterisk Talley is playing the round of her life.

Korda is the pre-tournament favorite and the World No. 1. But golf is played on the course, not the ranking sheet.

What a Sunday awaits at Riviera.
facebook.image_caption:
Korda. Kim. Talley. Hull. The USWO final round is HERE.

First U.S. Women's Open at Riviera. Sunday. Watch it all unfold. 🏆 #USWO2026 #LPGA

article.headline: Korda and Kim Share USWO Lead Heading Into Sunday at Riviera — With a 17-Year-Old Amateur in the Mix

stories.title: Memorial Tournament — Long Sunday at Muirfield Village; Poston and Gerard Tied at -9
stories.angle: R3 washed out Saturday, resumes Sunday 7:30 AM ET; Poston/Gerard tied at -9; leader and most of field still have holes to play in R3 before R4; Marathon Sunday at Muirfield Village; R4 tees off from 11 AM in groups of 3 off 2 tees

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Memorial Tournament Sunday is going to be a marathon.

R3 was rained out Saturday. Resumes 7:30 AM ET.

Poston and Gerard tied at -9. Both have 13 holes left in R3 before R4 starts.

Long day at Muirfield Village. #PGATour #MemorialTournament

facebook.long_form:
Window: 6:00 AM ET

The most grueling day of the PGA Tour season is unfolding at Muirfield Village Golf Club on Sunday.

Saturday's third round of the Memorial Tournament was suspended at 4:35 PM ET due to severe thunderstorms rolling through Dublin, Ohio. When J.T. Poston and Ryan Gerard — co-leaders at 9 under par — walked off the sixth green, play was called for the day. Both players have 13 holes remaining in their third round.

The schedule for Sunday: R3 resumes at 7:30 AM ET, with the final round beginning at 11 AM (groups of three off two tees through 12:45 PM).

That means Poston and Gerard could play upwards of 31 holes on Sunday — finishing their third round in the morning and immediately competing in the fourth. It is exactly the kind of physical and mental test that separates champions from contenders.

Poston has been the story of this tournament. His second round featured eight birdies, zero bogeys — a 65 that was 9 shots better than the field average on the same day. He is grinding beautifully.

Can he hold on through a full day of golf? Sunday will tell us.
facebook.image_caption:
R3 rained out. Poston and Gerard tied at -9 with 13 holes left.

Marathon Sunday at Muirfield Village. R3 resumes 7:30 AM ET. #PGATour #MemorialTournament

article.headline: Memorial Tournament Faces Marathon Sunday — Poston and Gerard Tied at -9 with 13 Holes Left in Round 3

stories.title: Asterisk Talley — 17-Year-Old Amateur Shoots 66, Makes USWO History
stories.angle: Talley (Fresno, CA) fired 5-under 66 in R3 — lowest round ever posted by an amateur on a weekend at the U.S. Women's Open; at 17 she is making history at Riviera; who is this phenom?

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Her name is Asterisk Talley.

She's 17 years old. She's from Fresno.

She shot 5-under 66 at Riviera on Saturday — the best amateur weekend round in U.S. Women's Open history.

Follow this name. #USWO2026 #LPGA

facebook.long_form:
Window: 10:30 AM ET

Here is a sentence you don't read every day: A 17-year-old amateur from Fresno, California just posted the best amateur weekend round in the history of the U.S. Women's Open.

Her name is Asterisk Talley. On Saturday at Riviera Country Club, she shot 5-under 66 — a number that would be exceptional for any professional player, let alone a teenager competing as an amateur in the country's most prestigious women's major.

The record she set — lowest amateur round on a weekend at the USWO — reflects the rarity of what she did. It is one thing to make the cut at this level. It is another thing entirely to shoot 66 on Saturday at Riviera and put your name on a leaderboard with the best players in the world.

Talley is playing Sunday. She is 17. She is an amateur.

This is why we watch golf.
facebook.image_caption:
Her name is Asterisk Talley. She's 17. She shot 66 on Saturday at Riviera.

The lowest amateur weekend round in U.S. Women's Open history. 🏌️‍♀️ #USWO2026

article.headline: Asterisk Talley, 17, Shoots 66 at Riviera — The Lowest Amateur Weekend Round in USWO History

stories.title: LIV Andalucia Final Round — Hatton Leads by 2, Garcia Chases in Front of Spanish Crowd
stories.angle: Hatton 10-under leads by 2 into Championship Sunday at Valderrama; Sergio Garcia in final group as crowd favorite; Thomas Detry in contention; Open Championship spot on the line

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Championship Sunday at Valderrama.

Hatton leads by 2 shots at -10.
Sergio Garcia in the final group.
Crowd behind Garcia in his home country.

This is what LIV Golf looks like at its best. #LIVGolf #Andalucia

facebook.long_form:
Window: 1:00 PM ET

Championship Sunday at Valderrama — one of the most iconic golf venues in Europe — and the leaderboard has produced the perfect final group.

Tyrrell Hatton leads by two shots at 10 under par, heading into the final round of LIV Golf Andalucia. The Englishman has been in this situation before: he led through 36 holes at LIV Golf Nashville in 2024 by four strokes, and won by six. His composure under pressure is well established.

But behind him in the final group is Sergio Garcia.

Garcia is not just a Spaniard at a Spanish tournament. He is Valderrama's most celebrated player — the man who has played this course hundreds of times and knows every slope, every wind angle, every way the course can punish you and reward you. The crowd on Sunday will be firmly, loudly in his corner.

Thomas Detry rounds out the final group, giving us three distinct narratives: the cool favorite, the beloved local hero, and the dark-horse contender.

An Open Championship exemption is among the prizes available to the winner.

Championship Sunday at Valderrama. This one has everything.
facebook.image_caption:
Hatton leads. Garcia chasing. Spanish crowd LOUD.

Championship Sunday at Valderrama. This is LIV Golf. 🏆 #LIVGolf #Andalucia

article.headline: Hatton Leads LIV Andalucia Into Championship Sunday — Garcia Chases at His Home Course

stories.title: Scheffler Grinds at Memorial — 10 Back, Still Fighting, Scouting Shinnecock
stories.angle: Scheffler made the cut despite admitting "worst swing in 2 years," 10 shots back; maintaining 75-consecutive-cut streak; scouting Shinnecock Hills for US Open June 18-21; Grand Slam quest still alive

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Scheffler says his swing is the worst it's been in two years.

He still made the cut. He's still at Muirfield Village. 75 consecutive cuts made.

US Open is in 11 days. He's already scouting Shinnecock.

Scottie Scheffler doesn't stop. #PGATour #MemorialTournament

facebook.long_form:
Window: 3:30 PM ET

Scottie Scheffler knows exactly what his swing looks like right now, and he is not happy about it.

"Worst swing I've had in two years," he said after navigating the Memorial Tournament's second round. He was 10 shots behind the leaders entering the weekend — a number that rarely produces champions.

But he made the cut. He always makes the cut. Sunday at Muirfield Village will mark the 75th consecutive PGA Tour cut he has survived — a streak of extraordinary consistency from a player who has won the Memorial Tournament in each of the past two seasons.

This week's Memorial is not going to produce a Scheffler three-peat. But that is almost beside the point. He is here. He is competing. And he is using this week as preparation for something larger.

The U.S. Open begins June 18 at Shinnecock Hills in Southampton, New York. Scheffler has been scouting the course, and the final round of the U.S. Open falls on June 21 — his 30th birthday.

He will not win the Memorial. He is going to win the round that counts on June 21.
facebook.image_caption:
Scheffler: "Worst swing in two years." Still made the cut.

75 consecutive cuts. Scouting Shinnecock. The US Open is 11 days away. #ScottieScheffler #PGATour

article.headline: Scheffler Grinds to Make Memorial Cut — 75 Consecutive Cuts and 11 Days to the U.S. Open

research.facts:
Scheffler at Memorial
**Scheffler situation (HIGH — CBS Sports, pipeline notes):**
- Made the cut at Memorial despite challenging week
- Sitting 10 shots back entering weekend
- Self-diagnosis: "Worst swing in two years" / "frustrating" (from pipeline June 6 notes)
- Cut streak: 75 consecutive PGA Tour cuts made (per pipeline notes)
**Scheffler US Open context (HIGH):**
- US Open: June 18-21 at Shinnecock Hills, Southampton, NY
- Scheffler scouting Shinnecock (per pipeline notes June 6)
- Grand Slam quest: needs US Open after Masters win and...
**Wait: Need to verify Scheffler Grand Slam context (MEDIUM):**
- From pipeline June 4: "Scheffler + McIlroy scouted Shinnecock (US Open June 18-21, Scheffler Grand Slam quest, June 21 = 30th birthday)"
- So Scheffler's 30th birthday is June 21 (final round of US Open)
- He has won Masters (2024-2025 three-peat mentioned in June 6: "Scheffler three-peat bid... only Tiger 1999-2001") — wait, actually June 4 says "Scheffler +310 three-peat bid" which is for the Memorial (he's a 3-time Memorial champ?) or for Masters?
From pipeline notes: "three-peat bid, same as Tiger 1999-2001" at Memorial. So Scheffler won Memorial 2024 AND 2025 and is going for 3-peat at Memorial.
So for US Open: Scheffler has likely won some majors and is chasing the Grand Slam (all 4 in one year) — a nearly impossible achievement. His birthday June 21 coincides with US Open final round.
**Scheffler Memorial context (HIGH from pipeline):**
- Won Memorial in 2024 and 2025 (three-peat attempt)
- Scheffler 75-consecutive-cut streak (very impressive)
- 10 shots back after rough weekend
